title: QUIC server-role secp256r1 HelloRetryRequest work item
summary: Add the bounded server-role HelloRetryRequest path for a retry-capable first ClientHello that advertises secp256r1 in supported_groups but omits a usable secp256r1 key_share, then rejoin the landed REQ-QUIC-CRT-0112 ServerHello floor on the retried ClientHello without widening the cryptographic subset.
planned_changes:
- Extend the server-role first-ClientHello parser/state machine to classify the retry-eligible branch where supported_groups includes secp256r1 but key_share omits a usable secp256r1 entry.
- Emit exactly one local HelloRetryRequest requesting secp256r1 on the existing Handshake-crypto output seam and keep ServerHello plus Handshake key publication unavailable until the retried ClientHello is accepted.
- Promptly retransmit the already-published HRR Initial packet when duplicate first-ClientHello Initial CRYPTO frames arrive after the HRR was sent and before Handshake keys exist, without producing a second TLS HelloRetryRequest.
- Rejoin the landed REQ-QUIC-CRT-0112 ServerHello and Handshake key-publication floor only when the retried ClientHello carries the requested secp256r1 key share.
- Add focused requirement-home tests for positive HelloRetryRequest emission, positive retried-ClientHello handoff into REQ-QUIC-CRT-0112, positive duplicate Initial HRR replay, negative no-secp256r1-supported_groups rejection, negative premature duplicate replay, negative repeated-retry rejection, and negative malformed/conflicting retried ClientHello handling.
- Add fuzz coverage for supported_groups/key_share ordering, duplication, and retry-branch state transitions.
- Extend permanent benchmarks over the server-side handshake parser/protection path and CRYPTO buffering overlap path so the retry classification and replay support do not become unmeasured hot-path changes.
verification_plan: Run focused requirement-home tests for positive first-ClientHello HelloRetryRequest emission, positive retried-ClientHello handoff into REQ-QUIC-CRT-0112, positive duplicate Initial HRR replay, and negative no-supported-group, premature duplicate, repeated-retry, and malformed/conflicting retried-ClientHello paths; replay the preserved quic-go first ClientHello evidence from `artifacts/interop-runner/20260422-110409619-server-nginx/` and duplicate-HRR-loss evidence from `artifacts/interop-runner/20260422-210022638-server-nginx/`; run fuzz coverage for supported_groups/key_share and duplicate-extension handling on the retry branch; run the permanent benchmark dry checks for the server-side handshake parser/protection and CRYPTO buffer overlap paths; then run the current CRT/INT guard set plus SpecTrace render and validation checks.
completion_notes: Implemented the bounded server-side secp256r1 HelloRetryRequest path plus packet-level replay of the already-published HRR Initial when live quic-go duplicate first-ClientHello Initials indicate likely HRR loss. The slice keeps the cryptographic floor pinned to TLS_AES_128_GCM_SHA256 over secp256r1 and does not add x25519, hybrid groups, or broader TLS group negotiation.
out_of_scope: certificate-flight changes beyond the existing server-role seam
opportunistic refactors unrelated to the retry branch
